title: Verify offline bundle signature during preflight (fail closed on invalid/untrusted)
preview: Context: As part of the Redwood Private on-prem/offline bundle signing initiative, the installer must verify bundle provenance before any install/upgrade steps run. This PR adds signature verification to the installer preflight and fails closed when the signature is invalid or the signing key is not trusted. This is...
text: Context: As part of the Redwood Private on-prem/offline bundle signing initiative, the installer must verify bundle provenance before any install/upgrade steps run. This PR adds signature verification to the installer preflight and fails closed when the signature is invalid or the signing key is not trusted. This is the first enforcement point; trust-store/rotation and break-glass flows will land in follow-up PRs. What this does: - Adds a new preflight gate `verify-offline-bundle-signature` that runs after bundle discovery but before unpack/apply. - Verifies that `bundle/manifest.json` is present and canonicalizable, and that it contains a signature envelope (`signing` block) produced by the release pipeline. - Verifies the signature over the canonical manifest bytes using Ed25519 (Go stdlib `crypto/ed25519`). - Validates trust via a single pinned Redwood release public key shipped wi...
links: Linear: ENG-4823 - Installer preflight verification and fail-closed behavior
Linear: ENG-4821 - Manifest schema + canonicalization rules
Follow-up: PR-24859 - Trust store and key rollover support

title: Selective component vendoring and federated-registry seed tool for air-gapped installs
preview: Motivation: customers running Redwood Private in highly constrained air-gapped sites asked for smaller, auditable offline bundles and a reproducible way to seed multiple local registries (federated seed) when performing staged rollouts across disconnected clusters. This PR introduces a selective component vendoring ...
text:
Motivation: customers running Redwood Private in highly constrained air-gapped sites asked for smaller, auditable offline bundles and a reproducible way to seed multiple local registries (federated seed) when performing staged rollouts across disconnected clusters. This PR introduces a selective component vendoring strategy and a small, focused registry-seed CLI that can bootstrap multiple registries from a shrinkwrapped manifest.

What this changes:
- Selective component vendoring: a new manifest layer (component-shrinkwrap.yaml) allows operators to opt-in to vendor only the runtime components they need (kubelet, containerd, cni, crictl, kernel-modules) instead of the entire distro image set. The installer will generate slim OCI bundles accordingly, reducing offline bundle size by up to ~60% in sample overlays.
- Federated registry seed tool: a new binary (installer/cmd/seed-registry...

title: operator-image-pinning-and-postrollback-validation-hooks
preview: Motivation: In several recent customer upgrade exercises we observed instability caused by operator images floating between upgrades and by silent chart metadata drift that allowed incompatible post-rollback states. This PR introduces three complementary safeguards: (1) operator image pinning via digest lockfiles su...
text:
Motivation: In several recent customer upgrade exercises we observed instability caused by operator images floating between upgrades and by silent chart metadata drift that allowed incompatible post-rollback states. This PR introduces three complementary safeguards: (1) operator image pinning via digest lockfiles surfaced in both the installer manifests and Terraform module outputs; (2) a chart sub-release stamping mechanism so the installer can detect and refuse upgrades that change sub-release semantics without an explicit migration plan; (3) a Helm post-rollback validation hook that runs lightweight data-plane health checks and fails the rollback (leaving operator in quarantined mode) if core invariants are violated.

What I changed (summary): Added an image lockfile generator and validation flow to the installer CLI; extended terraform/modules/installer to accept and export image_...

title: Add KMS preflight validation, permission checks, and audit-friendly validation report
preview: Context: We keep seeing Private installs fail late (or worse: succeed but later decrypt fails) due to KMS misconfiguration: wrong key ARN/region, missing IAM actions, key disabled, policy doesn’t allow the installer principal, or customer KMS throttling. This PR adds an explicit KMS preflight step to the Private ins...
text: Context: We keep seeing Private installs fail late (or worse: succeed but later decrypt fails) due to KMS misconfiguration: wrong key ARN/region, missing IAM actions, key disabled, policy doesn’t allow the installer principal, or customer KMS throttling. This PR adds an explicit KMS preflight step to the Private installer so we fail fast with actionable output, and we produce an audit-friendly validation report that customers can attach to change tickets/security evidence. Summary of changes (high level): 1) New installer preflight stage: "kms" runs by default when KMS envelope encryption is configured (and can also be invoked explicitly via `redwood-private-installer validate kms`). 2) Permission + configuration checks with actionable remediation: resolves KMS key identifier, validates region alignment, validates key state (enabled, not pending deletion), and performs a safe Encrypt/...

title: Introduce KMS Federation Operator + SealedSecret Guardian for cross-cluster HSM key propagation
preview: Context: customers deploying Redwood Private across multiple air-gapped datacenters requested automated, auditable propagation of HSM-wrapped keys and TLS certs between primary KMS instances and read-only local KMS proxies. This PR introduces a KMS Federation Operator (KFO) plus a SealedSecret Guardian daemonset to ...
text: Context: customers deploying Redwood Private across multiple air-gapped datacenters requested automated, auditable propagation of HSM-wrapped keys and TLS certs between primary KMS instances and read-only local KMS proxies. This PR introduces a KMS Federation Operator (KFO) plus a SealedSecret Guardian daemonset to orchestrate safe key material replication, reconciled by Terraform/Helm hooks. Motivation: avoid manual sealed secret unseal workflows during cluster join, reduce time-to-recovery for HSM failovers, and provide an auditable policy-based propagation model. Scope: operator CRDs + controller, Helm chart changes (upgrade hooks, post-install job), Terraform module for federation resources, upgrade/rollback safe hooks, docs and test harness. Checklist: [x] CRD and controller implementation; [x] Helm templates and upgrade-hook jobs; [x] Terraform module to provision federation rol...

title: vendor rke2 CNI plugins + offline upgrade/rollback hooks for air-gapped installs
preview: Motivation: Customers running Redwood Private on-prem with RKE2 often operate fully air-gapped clusters and require a bundled, validated CNI set plus deterministic upgrade/rollback behavior during offline upgrades. This PR vendors a minimal set of RKE2-compatible CNI artifacts (canal/flannel + calico dataplane bits ...
text: Motivation: Customers running Redwood Private on-prem with RKE2 often operate fully air-gapped clusters and require a bundled, validated CNI set plus deterministic upgrade/rollback behavior during offline upgrades. This PR vendors a minimal set of RKE2-compatible CNI artifacts (canal/flannel + calico dataplane bits and rke2-cni shim) into the offline bundle, adds a small SELinux module and policy packaging step required for RHEL environments, and introduces an idempotent offline upgrade/rollback hook framework so operators can perform safe rollbacks without internet access. What changed (high-level): 1) Packaging: added vendor/cni/ directory with compressed tarballs for canal and calico dataplane, rke2-cni shim, and SHA256 checksums. 2) Installer runtime: extend airgap preflight to verify CNI artifact signatures and space checks, and to unpack only the CNI payload needed by the target...

title: add-central-log-shipper-and-dashboard-migration-tooling-for-private-installs
preview: Adds a centralized log shipping option (promtail -> S3 -> loki indexer) as an optional subchart to the private-installer Helm bundle, a terraform module to provision long-term object storage and Grafana workspace configuration, and a one-shot dashboard migration utility that performs a schema-safe migration of exist...
text: Adds a centralized log shipping option (promtail -> S3 -> loki indexer) as an optional subchart to the private-installer Helm bundle, a terraform module to provision long-term object storage and Grafana workspace configuration, and a one-shot dashboard migration utility that performs a schema-safe migration of existing Grafana dashboards during upgrades. Motivation: customers deploying Private need a reliable, upgrade-safe path to connect cluster logs and persisted dashboards into the observability stack without manual intervention. This PR implements the Helm templates and values schema, the Terraform module that creates an S3-compatible bucket with lifecycle rules and IAM policy, a k8s Job-based migration runner that performs dashboard diffs and backouts, unit and e2e tests for the chart and migration tool, and documentation + an upgrade checklist. The description below summarizes c...

title: ci: generate release graph and manifest validator for private-installer artifacts
preview: Motivation: CI for private installer releases lacked a machine-readable release graph and a manifest-level validator that ties signed artifacts (images, helm charts, tf modules) back to the release manifest. This PR adds a release-graph generator and a manifest-validator stage to the private-installer CI pipeline to...
text: Motivation: CI for private installer releases lacked a machine-readable release graph and a manifest-level validator that ties signed artifacts (images, helm charts, tf modules) back to the release manifest. This PR adds a release-graph generator and a manifest-validator stage to the private-installer CI pipeline to ensure provenance, enable safer upgrades/rollbacks, and block unsigned/unaligned manifests from publishing. Summary of changes: - new pipeline step 'build:release-graph' which produces a directed release graph (JSON) that maps artifacts -> helm chart values -> terraform module versions and includes cryptographic digests and SBOM references; graph is collated from build outputs and attestation metadata - new pipeline step 'validate:manifest' that consumes a release graph and enforces: cosign signature verification (keyless and KMS), SBOM presence, chart values checksum, and...

title: external-kms-plugin-and-cert-sync-controller-for-onprem-rotation
preview: Motivation: private installations need a way to integrate external KMS (cloud or on-prem HSM gateways) without forcing Vault-as-first-class and to keep TLS artifacts synchronized across namespaces during key rotation. This PR introduces two coordinated changes: 1) an optional installer hook that deploys a lightweigh...
text: Motivation: private installations need a way to integrate external KMS (cloud or on-prem HSM gateways) without forcing Vault-as-first-class and to keep TLS artifacts synchronized across namespaces during key rotation. This PR introduces two coordinated changes: 1) an optional installer hook that deploys a lightweight external-kms-plugin sidecar and Terraform snippets to register external KMS endpoints (KMS plugin config + policy templates); 2) a cert-sync-controller (Kubernetes controller) that watches KMS-backed key metadata and existing TLS secrets (including sealed-secrets-wrapped secrets) to perform automated, atomic certificate replacements across the cluster during rotation. Goals: reduce manual rotation steps for Private deployments, support customer-managed KMS/HSM, and provide a safe upgrade path with canaries and rollbacks. The description below outlines implementation detai...

title: tenant-probe-injector-and-slo-synthesizer-for-private-installs
preview: Motivation and summary: This PR adds a tenant-aware probe injector and an SLO synthesizer to the Private Installer. The goal is to provide out-of-the-box synthetic checks and tenant-scoped SLOs/dashboards for Prometheus + Grafana in private/VPC deployments without forcing per-tenant manual configuration. This is tar...
text:
Motivation and summary: This PR adds a tenant-aware probe injector and an SLO synthesizer to the Private Installer. The goal is to provide out-of-the-box synthetic checks and tenant-scoped SLOs/dashboards for Prometheus + Grafana in private/VPC deployments without forcing per-tenant manual configuration. This is targeted at customers who want predictable SLOs and early detection of network or configuration regressions across tenant boundaries.

What it contains: 1) A Helm subchart 'probe-injector' that deploys a small sidecar injector controller and a configurable Prometheus Blackbox Exporter + probe CRDs. The sidecar injector can attach a lightweight HTTP/TCP/ICMP probe sidecar to tenant-facing pods in the private cluster via annotations applied by the installer. 2) An 'slo-synthesizer' component (Go binary + controller) that consumes tenant intent (SLO targets stored in a namespaced...

title: introduce-crossplane-driven-kms-provisioning-and-tls-orchestrator-for-onprem
preview:
Summary: Add a Crossplane-backed provisioning flow and a TLS orchestration component that coordinates HSM/KMS key lifecycle events with Helm/Terraform upgrades for private deployments.
Motivation: customers asked for an automated path to provision cloud-backed KMS instances (and on-prem HSM proxies) using Crossplane...
text:
Summary: Add a Crossplane-backed provisioning flow and a TLS orchestration component that coordinates HSM/KMS key lifecycle events with Helm/Terraform upgrades for private deployments.
Motivation: customers asked for an automated path to provision cloud-backed KMS instances (and on-prem HSM proxies) using Crossplane so the Private Installer can own end-to-end lifecycle for tenant keys during cluster bootstrapping and upgrades. This also reduces manual steps during TLS certificate rotations and prevents drift between sealed-secrets/Vault state and KMS key metadata.
Design: 1) crossplane/controller: new CRD 'KMSProvision' that maps to provider resources (AWS KMS, GCP KMS, or an HSM-proxy Deployment); 2) provisioning module: terraform shim and new Crossplane composition templates shipped in helm chart's templates/crossplane/; 3) tls-orchestrator daemonset: a small controller that watches...

title: Private observability: add preflight checks + post-install validation for Prometheus scrapes and Grafana dashboards
preview: ## Summary This PR adds installer-level observability guardrails for Redwood Private installs (VPC + on-prem), focused on the “Private observability minimum pack” rollout. It introduces (1) preflight checks to catch the most common misconfigurations that lead to blank dashboards / missing alerts (storage class, PVC ...
text:
## Summary This PR adds installer-level observability guardrails for Redwood Private installs (VPC + on-prem), focused on the “Private observability minimum pack” rollout. It introduces (1) preflight checks to catch the most common misconfigurations that lead to blank dashboards / missing alerts (storage class, PVC sizing, NetworkPolicy restrictions, resource requests) and (2) a post-install validation step that verifies Prometheus scrapes and Grafana dashboard import succeeded. The goal is to make installs more deterministic for customer platform teams and reduce Support escalations like SUP-49210.

## Motivation / context We’ve seen repeated failures where the installer completes successfully but the observability pack is effectively broken: Prometheus can’t scrape due to restrictive NetworkPolicies, Prometheus/Grafana PVCs can’t bind in customer clusters, or Grafana dashboards neve...

title: Include offline observability artifacts in air-gapped bundle + update manifest
preview: Context: Several on-prem / air-gapped Private installs reported Grafana coming up without any Redwood dashboards after install/upgrade because the offline bundle did not include the minimum-pack dashboard JSONs and Prometheus rules (SUP-49210, Slack thread: airgap-bundle-missing-grafana-dashboards). This PR updates ...
text: Context: Several on-prem / air-gapped Private installs reported Grafana coming up without any Redwood dashboards after install/upgrade because the offline bundle did not include the minimum-pack dashboard JSONs and Prometheus rules (SUP-49210, Slack thread: airgap-bundle-missing-grafana-dashboards). This PR updates the air-gap bundle build so the observability artifacts are shipped as first-class, versioned bundle contents. What changed: (1) add observability artifacts to the airgap bundle payload: Grafana dashboard bundle (runtime + control plane), Prometheus alerting/recording rules, and default log collector configs; (2) update the bundle manifest schema usage to explicitly list these artifacts under the 'artifacts' section with stable filenames and SHA256s; (3) wire extraction/install into post-unpack steps so upgrades can safely rehydrate dashboards/rules without requiring outbou...

title: Add installer docs for Terraform VPC module integration + improve network preflight errors
preview:
## Why
During design partner pilots we keep losing time to “is this VPC wired correctly?” ambiguity. We now have an alpha set of Terraform VPC modules in `redwood-terraform`, but the installer docs do not explain:
- what outputs the installer expects / how to map module outputs to installer config
- which VPC endpoi...
text:
## Why
During design partner pilots we keep losing time to “is this VPC wired correctly?” ambiguity. We now have an alpha set of Terraform VPC modules in `redwood-terraform`, but the installer docs do not explain:
- what outputs the installer expects / how to map module outputs to installer config
- which VPC endpoints + DNS settings are required for private-only installs
- how to interpret common failure modes (image pull failures, intermittent DNS)

This PR adds an explicit integration doc page in the installer repo, links out to the Terraform module suite, and tightens up a couple of preflight error messages so that support/SE can triage faster.

## What changed
### Docs
- Added a new guide: `docs/terraform-vpc-modules-integration.md`
 - Minimal “happy path” example wiring Terraform outputs into the installer config
 - Required endpoints list for fully-private installs (no public e...

title: Installer: detect required VPC endpoints + warn on missing ECR/private DNS
preview:
## Summary
This adds a network-focused preflight step to the Redwood Private installer to catch the most common failure mode we’ve seen in VPC-based pilots: image pulls failing because required interface endpoints (ECR API/DKR) are missing and/or the endpoint is present but **Private DNS is disabled**, causing the n...
text:
## Summary
This adds a network-focused preflight step to the Redwood Private installer to catch the most common failure mode we’ve seen in VPC-based pilots: image pulls failing because required interface endpoints (ECR API/DKR) are missing and/or the endpoint is present but **Private DNS is disabled**, causing the node to resolve the public ECR hostname.

The preflight is intentionally defensive: it attempts AWS-based detection when IAM permissions allow it, and otherwise falls back to DNS + connectivity heuristics. In both cases it produces targeted, copy/paste-able remediation guidance and links to the Terraform module docs.

## Motivation / Context
- Support + SE feedback shows we’re burning cycles late in the install when kubelet/containerd can’t pull images.
- In private subnet deployments with restricted egress, missing ECR endpoints is fatal.
- Even when endpoints exist, we’ve ...

title: Verify air-gapped upgrade bundles via SHA256 manifest + signature; improve missing/incorrect artifact errors
preview: Context: Design partners and Support have hit confusing failures in the offline/air-gapped upgrade flow when bundles are incomplete, corrupted, or built against the wrong target version (see SUP-18510). Today the installer will often fail late (during image/chart pull) and the error doesn’t clearly tell the operator...
text: Context: Design partners and Support have hit confusing failures in the offline/air-gapped upgrade flow when bundles are incomplete, corrupted, or built against the wrong target version (see SUP-18510). Today the installer will often fail late (during image/chart pull) and the error doesn’t clearly tell the operator what’s missing or mismatched. This PR adds deterministic bundle verification up front: (1) a required SHA256 checksum manifest for all bundle artifacts, (2) optional signature verification for the manifest, and (3) clearer, actionable error messages that enumerate missing/incorrect artifacts before any cluster mutations. Behavior: - New required file inside bundle: bundle.manifest.json (list of artifacts + expected sha256 + size + logical type). - New required file inside bundle: bundle.manifest.sha256 (sha256sum of bundle.manifest.json) OR embedded sha256 in the manifest ...

title: Add upgrade rollback command + persisted state machine
preview: Implements `redwood-private upgrade rollback` backed by a persisted upgrade state machine. The rollback command reads the last recorded upgrade attempt (plan + execution state), runs rollback hooks in a deterministic order (control plane first, then data plane), and verifies the cluster is healthy after rollback. Th...
text:
Implements `redwood-private upgrade rollback` backed by a persisted upgrade state machine. The rollback command reads the last recorded upgrade attempt (plan + execution state), runs rollback hooks in a deterministic order (control plane first, then data plane), and verifies the cluster is healthy after rollback. This is intended to make rollback an operator-safe, auditable action rather than a collection of manual kubectl/helm steps.

Context
- Follows PR #4821 (upgrade plan generator) and PR #4850 (backup orchestration + snapshot hooks).
- Designed for N-1 -> N upgrades with explicit rollback entry points for failures during plan execution.
- Addresses support incidents where upgrades failed during DB migrations and operators needed a consistent rollback procedure (SUP-18421, SUP-18488).

Operator UX
- Roll back the last attempted upgrade: `redwood-private upgrade rollback`
- Roll b...

title: Private upgrades: backup orchestrator + snapshot hooks (CSI/EBS) + optional logical backup
preview: Summary: This PR adds a first-class backup orchestration phase to the Private upgrade workflow. The installer now runs a pre-upgrade backup step that can (a) create volume snapshots via pluggable snapshotters (initial support: Kubernetes CSI VolumeSnapshot and AWS EBS snapshots via CSI annotations) and/or (b) perfor...
text: Summary: This PR adds a first-class backup orchestration phase to the Private upgrade workflow. The installer now runs a pre-upgrade backup step that can (a) create volume snapshots via pluggable snapshotters (initial support: Kubernetes CSI VolumeSnapshot and AWS EBS snapshots via CSI annotations) and/or (b) perform an optional logical DB backup (pg_dump) when snapshots are unavailable or when operators request an additional safety net. The orchestrator emits structured progress events (for CLI + future UI) and persists backup metadata into the upgrade state store so rollback and support bundles can reference what happened. Motivation: We’ve had multiple upgrade incidents/near-misses where rollback was risky because there was no deterministic backup step or because snapshot behaviors differed across environments. This change makes upgrades safe-by-default and reduces operator guesswo...

title: Private upgrades: add upgrade plan generator (staged plan + artifact validation + auditable summary)
preview: Summary This PR introduces an upgrade plan generator to `redwood-private-installer` as the first building block for the Private upgrade + rollback mechanism. The generator produces a staged plan (control-plane first, then data-plane), validates required artifacts before any changes are made, and writes an auditable ...
text: Summary This PR introduces an upgrade plan generator to `redwood-private-installer` as the first building block for the Private upgrade + rollback mechanism. The generator produces a staged plan (control-plane first, then data-plane), validates required artifacts before any changes are made, and writes an auditable plan artifact + human-readable summary to disk. Motivation/Context We need a safe-by-default workflow for Redwood Private upgrades (VPC + on-prem/air-gapped) with deterministic execution, clear failure modes, and artifacts that can be attached to support bundles and audit logs. The plan generator is intentionally separated from execution so operators (and Release Eng/SRE) can review what will happen before they run it. This aligns with ENG-4821 and the rollout UX direction in DES-311. What’s included - New CLI: `redwood-private upgrade plan` - Produces a versioned plan arti...

title: Implement pluggable preflight check framework for Private upgrades
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a pluggable preflight check framework in the Private installer. The framework standardizes check execution, result reporting, severity levels, and remediation pointers so upgrades are safer-by-default and failures are actionable for operators. Context: Part of the “Private upgrade + rollb...
text: Summary: This PR introduces a pluggable preflight check framework in the Private installer. The framework standardizes check execution, result reporting, severity levels, and remediation pointers so upgrades are safer-by-default and failures are actionable for operators. Context: Part of the “Private upgrade + rollback mechanism” effort. This PR focuses on the framework + CLI surface for preflight runs; follow-up PRs will add/expand the actual checks (cluster health, storage, RBAC/SSO reachability, artifact availability, etc.). Goals: (1) Make prechecks composable (registerable plugins) and deterministic, (2) Provide consistent UX for operators and Support, (3) Establish blocking vs warning semantics that can be used by upgrade plan generation and change-management gating. Design notes: Checks are registered by ID and grouped into named phases (environment, control-plane, data-plane, ...

title: Wire up RBAC v2 admin bootstrap for Private installs (initial role bindings + IdP group mapping docs)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a safe, deterministic “admin bootstrap” path for RBAC v2 in Redwood Private installs to prevent lockout under deny-by-default. On first install/upgrade (when RBAC v2 is enabled), the installer will create an initial role binding granting a built-in admin role to a customer-specified subject (em...
text:
Summary: This PR adds a safe, deterministic “admin bootstrap” path for RBAC v2 in Redwood Private installs to prevent lockout under deny-by-default. On first install/upgrade (when RBAC v2 is enabled), the installer will create an initial role binding granting a built-in admin role to a customer-specified subject (email or IdP group). This also documents the recommended approach for mapping IdP groups (SAML/OIDC) to Redwood roles in Private.

Context / Motivation: As we roll RBAC v2 into Private, new clusters can end up with RBAC enforcement enabled before any role bindings exist, resulting in a hard lockout where no identity has permissions to create the first role binding. Hosted/Dedicated can rely on internal provisioning workflows; Private needs an install-time “seed” that is explicit, auditable, and easy for customers to configure. This PR implements the minimum bootstrap mechanis...

title: Private installer: pinning requirements for Runtime LTS artifacts (checksums, digests, rollback)
preview: Context: This PR is primarily a requirements/alignment thread captured in the PR description + review comments to unblock implementation of Runtime LTS pinning in the Redwood Private installer. Dedicated/Private enterprise customers want: (1) the ability to pin a specific runtime patch (e.g., 1.18.1-lts) for 6–12+ m...
text:
Context: This PR is primarily a requirements/alignment thread captured in the PR description + review comments to unblock implementation of Runtime LTS pinning in the Redwood Private installer. Dedicated/Private enterprise customers want: (1) the ability to pin a specific runtime patch (e.g., 1.18.1-lts) for 6–12+ months, (2) verifiable artifacts (checksums/signatures/provenance) that can be audited, and (3) a predictable rollback path when a patch introduces regressions. This PR documents and finalizes the installer-facing requirements so Release Eng can publish the right metadata and Runtime can keep semantics stable across the LTS line.

Requirements agreed in this thread:
- Pin semantics (must be explicit):
 - Installer MUST support pinning to an exact LTS patch version string (e.g., runtime=1.18.1-lts) and MUST NOT silently upgrade within the LTS line.
 - Installer SHOULD optiona...

title: Wire retention defaults into Private install + document purge job backing services
preview:
Title: Wire retention defaults into Private install + document purge job backing services

Context
Private customers (regulated workloads) are asking two recurring questions during security reviews: (1) what are the default retention behaviors when they install Redwood Private, and (2) what infrastructure is require...
text:
Title: Wire retention defaults into Private install + document purge job backing services

Context
Private customers (regulated workloads) are asking two recurring questions during security reviews: (1) what are the default retention behaviors when they install Redwood Private, and (2) what infrastructure is required to reliably enforce purges (scheduled jobs, storage/indexing dependencies, and verification signals). This PR updates the private installer to ship private-friendly defaults for retention + audit logging and adds a dedicated doc section describing purge job prerequisites.

Scope
- Set secure retention defaults in the installer Helm values (aligned to retention controls v1): conservative request/response log retention, longer audit log retention, and explicit “disabled logging” behavior where applicable.
- Enable purge job runner + recommended throttles by default (idempot...

title: Private installer: add backup format versioning, restore compatibility checks, and migration/operator messaging
preview: Context: We’ve had a few confusing restore failures in staging and in at least one on-prem customer scenario (SUP-18477) where the operator did not realize the backup artifact was produced by an older installer/runtime and required either (a) a restore-time migration step, or (b) a compatible installer version. This...
text:
Context: We’ve had a few confusing restore failures in staging and in at least one on-prem customer scenario (SUP-18477) where the operator did not realize the backup artifact was produced by an older installer/runtime and required either (a) a restore-time migration step, or (b) a compatible installer version. This PR introduces explicit backup format versioning and a restore-time compatibility gate, plus clearer operator-facing errors and a short “migration notes” doc to reduce Support/SRE escalation time.

What this PR does (high level): 1) Adds a required `format_version` field to the backup manifest and embeds it into the artifact metadata, 2) Adds compatibility checks early in `restore apply` (before doing any destructive actions), including minimum/maximum supported versions and a readable “why” message, 3) Introduces explicit error codes and remediation hints (upgrade/downgrad...

title: Introduce DNS-forwarder shadowing and deterministic SG naming for private control plane
preview: Motivation: Private control plane rollouts sometimes experience DNS transient failure during cross-account peering and SG rotation. This PR introduces a lightweight, opt-in "shadowed" DNS forwarder that mirrors control plane DNS zones into a customer-managed private hosted zone and a deterministic security-group nam...
text: Motivation: Private control plane rollouts sometimes experience DNS transient failure during cross-account peering and SG rotation. This PR introduces a lightweight, opt-in "shadowed" DNS forwarder that mirrors control plane DNS zones into a customer-managed private hosted zone and a deterministic security-group naming scheme to make policy and rotation idempotent across re-applies. What changed: Terraform module additions (control-plane/dns-forwarder, network/sg-naming); Helm chart values to enable the forwarder as a DaemonSet for on-prem installs; an upgrade job that creates provisional shadow zones and validates DNS propagation; a small operator-side hook for safe SG swaps during migration. Rollout plan: Default behavior is unchanged. Operators can enable the feature via the private-installer helm values or by setting enable_shadow_dns = true in the Terraform module. Rollout steps ...

title: offline-bootstrap: OCI index + terraform mirror module for air-gapped registry bootstrapping
preview: Motivation: customers deploying Redwood Private in fully air-gapped datacenters requested a more deterministic bootstrap flow for registries and OS images. Current airgap bundles are large and monolithic which makes incremental updates and signature verification awkward during upgrades. This PR introduces a compact ...
text: Motivation: customers deploying Redwood Private in fully air-gapped datacenters requested a more deterministic bootstrap flow for registries and OS images. Current airgap bundles are large and monolithic which makes incremental updates and signature verification awkward during upgrades. This PR introduces a compact OCI image index format for runtime bundles, and a Terraform module to provision a local mirror + registry-signing step in offline environments. Changes include: 1) a new 'oci-index' manifest generator that emits per-arch component descriptors (runtime images, charts, kernel modules) and an LRU-aware delta manifest pointer for efficient staged upgrades; 2) a Terraform module redwood-terraform/registry-mirror which wires up a local registry, preloads index manifests, and produces a signed admission config used by the installer; 3) Helm chart hooks to verify image signatures u...

title: Add `restore apply` workflow with preflight checks and post-restore validation report
preview: Implements the end-to-end restore path for Redwood Private in the installer CLI. This adds a new `restore apply` command that: (1) performs strict preflight checks (cluster connectivity, namespace/CRD readiness, version compatibility, storage backend access, and integrity checks on the backup artifact), (2) restores...
text: Implements the end-to-end restore path for Redwood Private in the installer CLI. This adds a new `restore apply` command that: (1) performs strict preflight checks (cluster connectivity, namespace/CRD readiness, version compatibility, storage backend access, and integrity checks on the backup artifact), (2) restores control-plane state in a deterministic order (cluster prerequisites, control-plane chart values/config, DB restore, then service rollout), and (3) runs post-restore health checks and emits a validation report that Support/SRE can use for escalation. The goal is to make restores repeatable, safer to run under time pressure, and easier to debug when something goes wrong. Restore ordering is intentionally conservative to avoid the on-prem issues we saw in SUP-18477 (missing CRDs/incorrect bootstrap order). Security note: we do not restore secret material from backups; we rest...

title: Support air-gapped backup artifact import/export (offline mode + local checksum verification)
preview: ## Summary This PR adds first-class support for moving backup artifacts into/out of air-gapped environments with deterministic, locally verifiable integrity checks. It introduces: (1) `backup export` to package a previously created backup artifact + manifest into a portable bundle directory (or tar), (2) `backup imp...
text: ## Summary This PR adds first-class support for moving backup artifacts into/out of air-gapped environments with deterministic, locally verifiable integrity checks. It introduces: (1) `backup export` to package a previously created backup artifact + manifest into a portable bundle directory (or tar), (2) `backup import` to ingest a bundle into an air-gapped target cluster/operator workstation without any outbound calls, and (3) an explicit `--offline` mode that hard-fails if code paths attempt network access (including accidental cloud metadata calls). The goal is to enable on-prem/air-gapped customers to follow a repeatable workflow: create backup -> export to removable media -> import on restore site -> restore apply. Related: ENG-9829 (air-gapped bundle signing/verification follow-ups), ENG-9821 (`backup create`), customer issue context in SUP-18477 (restore failures due to missing...

title: Add envelope encryption KMS provider interface (AWS reference impl + secure defaults)
preview: Context: Private Day-2 backup/restore requires encryption at rest with customer-managed keys where applicable. The installer currently produces backup artifacts (tarball + manifest), but encryption is not pluggable and we can’t support customer KMS/HSM constraints cleanly across VPC + on-prem. This PR introduces a s...
text:
Context: Private Day-2 backup/restore requires encryption at rest with customer-managed keys where applicable. The installer currently produces backup artifacts (tarball + manifest), but encryption is not pluggable and we can’t support customer KMS/HSM constraints cleanly across VPC + on-prem. This PR introduces a small, testable abstraction for KMS-based envelope encryption and ships a single reference provider implementation for AWS KMS, with secure defaults and explicit failure modes.

What’s in this PR: (1) New `kms.Provider` interface (GenerateDataKey/DecryptDataKey) that supports KMS-backed wrapping/unwrapping of a per-backup DEK (data encryption key). (2) New `crypto/envelope` package that performs streaming AES-256-GCM encryption of backup payloads using a DEK, storing only the wrapped key + nonce + algorithm metadata in the backup manifest. (3) AWS reference provider using `k...

title: Add upgrade preflight gate + rollback hints for enterprise private upgrades
preview: Context: Private upgrades were relying on tribal knowledge for preflight checks and rollback readiness. This PR adds a first-class `upgrade preflight` command and standardizes the minimum gates we expect before an enterprise window starts. The intent is to align the tool output with the Confluence go/no-go checklist...
text:
Context: Private upgrades were relying on tribal knowledge for preflight checks and rollback readiness. This PR adds a first-class `upgrade preflight` command and standardizes the minimum gates we expect before an enterprise window starts. The intent is to align the tool output with the Confluence go/no-go checklist and the Change Management policy.

What changed: (1) New command `rpi upgrade preflight` that runs cluster readiness, API deprecation checks, cert expiry checks, and audit-log sink reachability checks. (2) Adds `--evidence-file` flag to dump a text report for attachment to a Jira change record. (3) Adds inline rollback hints based on what was changed (control plane vs data plane).

Operator-facing behavior: The preflight exits non-zero on hard blockers. Warnings are printed for non-blocking findings and are intended to be explicitly acknowledged by the change owner.

Check...

title: private-installer: add `backup create` command (manifest + encrypted artifact + verification output)
preview: This PR introduces an initial `backup create` workflow in the Redwood Private installer CLI to support Day-2 operations and DR readiness. The command produces (1) a human-readable manifest (JSON) describing what was captured and how it was encrypted, and (2) an encrypted artifact (tarball) containing the control-pla...
text: This PR introduces an initial `backup create` workflow in the Redwood Private installer CLI to support Day-2 operations and DR readiness. The command produces (1) a human-readable manifest (JSON) describing what was captured and how it was encrypted, and (2) an encrypted artifact (tarball) containing the control-plane state snapshot files and selected deterministic configuration inputs. The goal of this PR is to ship the core mechanics (consistent snapshot + packaging + encryption + basic verification/logging) so Solutions/SRE teams can begin rehearsing restores in staging while we iterate on storage backends, scheduled jobs, and restore automation. Scope: control-plane backup only (no data-plane model cache / no customer prompt data). Secrets are not exported as raw secret material; we only record references (e.g., external KMS key ARNs, secret names) and require the target cluster t...

title: add-oci-prefetcher-and-kubelet-ephemeral-overlay-for-airgapped-bootstrap
preview:
Problem: air-gapped installs often spend excessive time pulling large runtime images and kernel modules during initial bootstrap. Existing delta snapshots help, but cluster bring-up still faces long cold starts and fragile manual registry seeding.

Solution: introduce an OCI prefetcher + index format plus a thin eph...
text:
Problem: air-gapped installs often spend excessive time pulling large runtime images and kernel modules during initial bootstrap. Existing delta snapshots help, but cluster bring-up still faces long cold starts and fragile manual registry seeding.

Solution: introduce an OCI prefetcher + index format plus a thin ephemeral kubelet overlay that allows the installer to stream and stage only required image layers and kubelet artifacts into a temporary local store before handing control to the normal runtime. Key components in this PR: (1) installer/terraform module to create a prefetcher DaemonSet and registry proxy for sealed bundles; (2) a compact OCI index format and tooling (installer/tools/oci-indexer) that produces layered prefetch manifests optimized for minimal disk churn; (3) helm chart additions that render a kubelet-ephemeral overlay (charts/redwood-private/templates/kubelet-ep...

title: manifest rollup + attestation gating: canonical index and CI enforcement
preview: Motivation: Private-installer releases need a single canonical manifest index that the CI can attest and gate on before publishing to customers. This PR implements a manifest rollup transformer that ingests per-arch/per-release artifact metadata, computes a deterministic canonical index (content-addressed ordering +...
text:
Motivation: Private-installer releases need a single canonical manifest index that the CI can attest and gate on before publishing to customers. This PR implements a manifest rollup transformer that ingests per-arch/per-release artifact metadata, computes a deterministic canonical index (content-addressed ordering + delta graph), emits an attestable bundle, and wires an optional CI gating check that rejects a release if attestations are missing or provenance fails verification.

This is intended to reduce the number of manual steps in private-installer release runs, make rollbacks deterministic (by publishing a signed index snapshot), and give SREs/Customers cryptographic proof of the exact artifacts applied by an upgrade. Related Linear: ENG-4379 (manifest rollup) and ENG-4391 (attestation policy).

What this PR does (high level):
- Adds manifest-rollup binary and library used in the...

title: transactional-release-apply: add TUF-backed transactional locks and manifest staging
preview: Problem: release application for private-installer can partially apply manifests when concurrent pipelines overlap (e.g. two builds push near-simultaneously). This has caused transient mismatches between the published release manifest and the in-cluster helm state, forcing manual rollbacks. Goal: make release manife...
text:
Problem: release application for private-installer can partially apply manifests when concurrent pipelines overlap (e.g. two builds push near-simultaneously). This has caused transient mismatches between the published release manifest and the in-cluster helm state, forcing manual rollbacks. Goal: make release manifest publishing and cluster apply transactional and idempotent so that either the entire manifest set is applied (and published) or nothing changes, with a short, observable staging window and an automatic rollback marker if something fails.

What this PR does (single string): Adds a TUF-backed transactional lock and a manifest staging area to the private-installer release pipeline. The new flow stages a release bundle under a provisional manifest name (manifest:vN-staging), obtains a transactional advisory lock via the TUF metadata store (using conditional metadata updates a...

title: implement-tf-module-gpu-nodepool-lease-index-and-iam-role-template-rotation
preview: Context and motivation: Private deployments need consistent, deterministic allocation of GPU nodepools across tenants while preserving immutability in IAM role configuration for auditability. Current tenant lease allocation can drift when nodes are recreated or TF state is sharded, causing tenant-to-node affinity re...
text:
Context and motivation: Private deployments need consistent, deterministic allocation of GPU nodepools across tenants while preserving immutability in IAM role configuration for auditability. Current tenant lease allocation can drift when nodes are recreated or TF state is sharded, causing tenant-to-node affinity regressions and noisy autoscaler behavior. This PR introduces a new Terraform module that provides a deterministic `lease_index` allocator for GPU nodepools and an IAM role templating system that creates immutable role versions with automated rotation hooks.

What this PR does: 1) Adds module `tfmodules/gpu_lease_index` implementing a lease-index allocation strategy that: computes a stable index for tenant leases based on tenant-id hashing + ordered subnet slices; emits outputs consumed by the nodepool provisioning module to ensure stable assignment even during partial applie...

title: introduce-interactive-migration-safepoints-and-rollback-fence
preview: Motivation: Private deployments need a safer, operator-driven way to perform schema migrations and rollbacks across multi-tenant clusters. Previous upgrades occasionally left clusters in a state where a naive downgrade could break tenant DB schemas or leave a partial logical replication setup. This PR introduces (1)...
text:
Motivation: Private deployments need a safer, operator-driven way to perform schema migrations and rollbacks across multi-tenant clusters. Previous upgrades occasionally left clusters in a state where a naive downgrade could break tenant DB schemas or leave a partial logical replication setup. This PR introduces (1) an interactive safepoint API exposed by the operator to mark tenant migration safepoints, (2) a rollback-fence that prevents downgrades when incompatible schema delta exists, and (3) a set of Helm and Terraform guard checks to ensure plan/upgrade parity before applying changes.

Summary of changes (single-line): interactive safepoint operator API, rollback-fence enforcement, helm preflight hook to quiesce workloads, timeline-aware postgres snapshotter and restore verifier, terraform state version gate.

Checklist (for merge):
- [x] Operator: add Safepoint CRD + controller ...

title: airgap: kubelet provisioning + OCI registry mirroring + reproducible delta bundles
preview: Motivation: customers running fully air-gapped Private deployments reported two recurring pain points: (1) manual kubelet/node provisioning steps that vary by distro, and (2) brittle offline registry mirroring that broke during incremental upgrades. This PR delivers: 1) a cross-distro kubelet provisioning helper tha...
text: Motivation: customers running fully air-gapped Private deployments reported two recurring pain points: (1) manual kubelet/node provisioning steps that vary by distro, and (2) brittle offline registry mirroring that broke during incremental upgrades. This PR delivers: 1) a cross-distro kubelet provisioning helper that generates systemd unit snippets + containerd mirror configs from the airgap catalog and injects them into the node bootstrap bundle; 2) an OCI registry mirror manifest generator that produces deterministic image manifest lists and mirror index files so mirror replication can be validated offline; 3) a reproducible delta-bundle generator that emits compact binary diffs between runtime release bundles to reduce s3/usb transfer sizes and speed upgrades in low-bandwidth datacenters; 4) an upgrade/rollback hook that stages kubelet config changes and can atomically swap systemd...

title: atomic-kms-key-promotion-with-grace-period-rollback-hooks
preview: Motivation: On private deployments customers run HSM-backed KMS with periodic key rotations. Past rotations that involved immediate primary key swaps caused brief failures during leader election and sealed-secrets unseal handoffs in air-gapped clusters. This PR implements an "atomic key promotion" flow plus a config...
text: Motivation: On private deployments customers run HSM-backed KMS with periodic key rotations. Past rotations that involved immediate primary key swaps caused brief failures during leader election and sealed-secrets unseal handoffs in air-gapped clusters. This PR implements an "atomic key promotion" flow plus a configurable grace period and automated rollback hooks to ensure safe cutover without downtime.
